title: Some news regarding the English VOs
display_title: Some news regarding the English VOs
wikitext:
[]: Some news regarding the English VOs             Quote Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Sun Sep 06, 2020 2:24 pm Evening everyone Last night I had a lovely chat with spike spencer, and I asked him about the final movie, and to my surprise sort of he confirmed that as it stands no English rebuild actor is signed on to do this final movie and it will all depend on funimation and if they acquire the rights to do the final film Which is fair enough. Even more interesting is that he’s so out of the loop and probably no overly keeping up with news that he didn’t even know that it’s now been given the title thrice upon a time, he was very confused and didn’t know what to make of it. But anyways that’s what I found out from our chat. He also hopes that this final film will see a happy closure for the characters and doesn’t wanna see a similar ending as was in the end of evangelion.

[]: Re: Some news regarding the English VOs             Quote Post by FelipeFritschF » Sun Sep 06, 2020 4:03 pm Thanks, this is interesting information. I've heard conflicting sources on Funimation owning the licence for all four movies or just each movie individually. Of course, even though Khara seems dissatisfied at them, there's not really a viable alternative to distribute their movie internationally, or more precisely in US theaters, as AFAIK Crunchyroll and the like don't have that capacity. Even so we don't really know if they'd impose different conditions like they imposed the 3.0 redub or if they would have closer oversight like they did for it as well as the Netflix NGE release - or if they would use the same VAs, having cast them in that instance. Of course, the Rebuilds had most of its cast changed 13 years ago and the remaining ADV VAs are very different now than how they were in 1995. So they might not change them again for the last of four movies.

[]: Re: Some news regarding the English VOs             Quote Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Sun Sep 06, 2020 5:14 pm Unless Netflix buys up the licence lol then we are in a world of shit in terms of the dub.

[]: Re: Some news regarding the English VOs             Quote Post by dzzthink » Mon Sep 07, 2020 11:58 am I really disliked listening to the netflix dub (did alright in some areas but nothing special). The original VO were great and I even waited a ridiculously long time for the funimation 3.33 dub. It would definitely ruin the continuity and my movie experience.

Article: Some news regarding the English VOs
[]: Some news regarding the English VOs             Quote Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Sun Sep 06, 2020 2:24 pm Evening everyone Last night I had a lovely chat with spike spencer, and I asked him about the final movie, and to my surprise sort of he confirmed that as it stands no English rebuild actor is signed on to do this final movie and it will all depend on funimation and if they acquire the rights to do the final film Which is fair enough. Even more interesting is that he’s so out of the loop and probably no overly keeping up with news that he didn’t even know that it’s now been given the title thrice upon a time, he was very confused and didn’t know what to make of it. But anyways that’s what I found out from our chat. He also hopes that this final film will see a happy closure for the characters and doesn’t wanna see a similar ending as was in the end of evangelion.

Of course, even though Khara seems dissatisfied at them, there's not really a viable alternative to distribute their movie internationally, or more precisely in US theaters, as AFAIK Crunchyroll and the like don't have that capacity. Even so we don't really know if they'd impose different conditions like they imposed the 3.0 redub or if they would have closer oversight like they did for it as well as the Netflix NGE release - or if they would use the same VAs, having cast them in that instance. Of course, the Rebuilds had most of its cast changed 13 years ago and the remaining ADV VAs are very different now than how they were in 1995. So they might not change them again for the last of four movies.
